name: transcript-path-probe
purpose: R10 precondition (tasks.md T004): the hook payload's transcript_path must be distinct per agent instance, or actor-key derivation needs a different signal.
provenance: Recorded from Claude Code hook-payload behavior (docs + observed layout): each agent instance — the main session and every Task-spawned subagent — writes its own transcript file under the project's transcript directory; hook events fired by a subagent's tool calls carry that subagent's own transcript_path, not the main session's.
residual_risk: If a future runtime version funnels subagent hook events through the main transcript_path, all actors collapse to one key: the turn cap would meter the pooled session rather than the triage agent alone, and trace attribution loses per-agent detail. Consequence is bounded (fail-open: unregistered actors are uncapped; nothing blocks), and slice 6's spawn-time role registration is the natural place to re-probe live.
finding: distinct transcript_path per agent instance while session_id stays shared — actor key = hash-suffix of transcript_path is a valid identity signal (R10 holds).
